guidance.title: Traditional guidance
guidance.disclaimer: Traditional guidance only; not medical, legal, financial, safety, or professional advice.

activities.ruling: Best suited to important decisions, leadership work, commitments, presentations, and decisive starts.
activities.eating: Good for steady productive work, learning, planning, support, purchases, and tasks that need nourishment or accumulation.
activities.walking: Use for ordinary movement, errands, communication, follow-ups, routine travel, and practical tasks.
activities.sleeping: Keep to rest, review, maintenance, quiet preparation, and low-risk routine work; avoid major launches.
activities.dying: Traditionally avoided for new beginnings, commitments, travel starts, and risky work; use for stopping, clearing, or closing tasks.

effects.very_good: Strongest signal; prefer this when timing matters.
effects.good: Supportive; suitable when a very favourable period is not available.
effects.average: Neutral; fine for ordinary tasks, weak for important starts.
effects.very_bad: Weakest; avoid high-importance actions where possible.

disclaimer.text: Pancha Pakshi is a traditional astrological system. The information shown here is provided for cultural and informational purposes and is not scientifically validated. It must not replace medical, legal, financial, safety or other professional advice.

about.title: About
about.body: Fernando Family Astrology is an open-source platform for traditional astrology tools, built to be precise, bilingual, and transparent about how every number is calculated. The first tool is a live Pancha Pakshi timetable, calculated from real sunrise and sunset times for your exact location rather than a fixed schedule. Every calculation is powered by an openly licensed, version-pinned engine, and the full source code is public.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.noAccounts: Accounts are optional. Everything works without signing in, with all data kept on your device. An invite-only Google sign-in exists for syncing saved profiles and account defaults across devices.
privacy.birthDataHandling: Birth details and precise location you enter are used only to compute the result you asked for. They are never stored on the server, never written to logs, never sent to analytics, and never included in a shareable link.
privacy.localStorageBody: Only non-sensitive preferences are saved locally on your device: your chosen language, light/dark theme, last-selected bird, a short list of recent locations, and the last successfully calculated schedule (so it can be shown while offline). None of this is birth date, birth time, or precise coordinates tied to a real event.
privacy.clearAction: You can remove everything saved locally at any time.
privacy.accountsBody: If you sign in (currently invite-only), your saved profiles and preferences sync to the server so they follow you across devices. Profiles store only a label plus the derived bird — or nakshatra and paksha. Account defaults may store language, theme, default bird, and a default location you chose. Raw birth date, time, and event-specific coordinates are never stored on the server, signed in or not.
privacy.pushBody: If you enable period alerts, our server stores this device's push address, your bird (or nakshatra and paksha), a location rounded to roughly 1 km, and your alert settings — never your birth date, time or exact coordinates. Disabling alerts deletes all of it immediately.
privacy.locationBody: If you use "Use my location," your coordinates are read by your browser after you explicitly grant permission and resolved to a timezone on your device. To show a readable place name instead of a generic label, those coordinates are also sent to OpenStreetMap's Nominatim service to look up a name — this only happens after your click, and only for that lookup; if it fails, a generic label is shown instead. Searching for a place by name sends only the text you typed to a different geocoding service (Open-Meteo), never your device's location.

licensing.title: Licence
licensing.body: This entire repository is licensed under the GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 (AGPL-3.0). The Pancha Pakshi calculation engine is powered by PyJHora, pinned to release V4.8.7 (commit ca22995), also AGPL-3.0. Because this is a network service built on AGPL-3.0 code, the corresponding source for exactly what's running is always available from the public repository linked below.
licensing.repoLinkLabel: View full licensing details and source code

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.sunriseDay: A Pancha Pakshi day runs from sunrise to the next sunrise, not from midnight to midnight. Sunrise, sunset, and the following sunrise are calculated precisely for your exact latitude, longitude, and timezone.
methodology.birdMapping: Your birth bird is derived from your birth Nakshatra and Paksha (waxing or waning moon), following the traditional five-bird mapping, or can be selected directly if your birth details aren't known.
methodology.periods: Each day is divided into 10 major periods (5 during the day, 5 at night) and 50 detailed sub-periods (5 within each major period), sourced from the pinned calculation engine's reference data — never invented or manually entered.
methodology.timezones: All calculations use IANA timezones and account for daylight-saving transitions on the specific date requested.
methodology.limitations: This is a traditional system provided for cultural and informational purposes; see the disclaimer for its limits. Activity guidance explains common traditional usage of the five activities and effect levels; the actual effect and rating still come from the pinned source table.
methodology.panchangaAyanamsa: Sidereal positions for tithi, nakshatra, yoga, karana, and the other panchanga elements use the calculation engine's default ayanamsa (a Lahiri-family standard), the same one Pancha Pakshi's schedules already rely on. Different published almanacs sometimes use a slightly different ayanamsa, which can shift an element's end time by a few minutes — a known difference between traditions, not an error.
methodology.panchangaPoyaRule: Sinhala Poya (full-moon) days and the Poya-cycle month names are computed astronomically, then checked against all 73 officially gazetted Sri Lankan Poya days from 2021 to 2026 — the computed rule matches every one of them.
methodology.panchangaDivergence: One date, 30 May 2026, is a documented exception: that year's official gazette names the Poya differently from what this engine computes, because of a difference in how each treats that year's extra intercalary month. See the technical calculation notes for detail.
methodology.sourceLinkLabel: Read the full calculation methodology in the source repository

landing.description: Open-source bilingual astrology platform with an accurate live Pancha Pakshi timetable calculated from real sunrise and sunset times.

panchanga.showingDefaults: Showing today for the default location — change date or location above.

compatibility.title: Bird Compatibility
compatibility.description: Choose two Pancha Pakshi birds to compare their relationship in the pinned source tables.
compatibility.contextDependent: This pair appears with more than one relationship in the source tables. The headline uses the most common relationship.
compatibility.note: This tool compares direct bird pairs only. It does not use birth data, location, date, or account state.
